title: Richard Zeckhauser on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Richard Zeckhauser, Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy, at Harvard Kennedy School, sits down with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) to talk about everything from how to test your optimism to eating salmon to paltering, and saving the Air Force money.
body: Richard Zeckhauser is the Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy, Kennedy School, Harvard University. He graduated from Harvard College (summa cum laude) and received his Ph.D. there. He is an elected fellow of the Econometric Society, the Institute of Medicine (National Academy of Sciences), and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. In 2014, he was named a Distinguished Fellow of the American Economic Association. His contributions to decision theory and behavioral economics include the concepts of quality-adjusted life years (QALYs), status quo bias, betrayal aversion, and ignorance (states of the world unknown) as a complement to the categories of risk and uncertainty. Many of his policy investigations explore ways to promote the health of human beings, to help markets work more effectively, and to foster informed and appropriate choices by individuals and government agencies. Zeckhauser has published over 300 articles. His recent coauthored books are The Patron's Payoff: Conspicuous Commissions in Italian Renaissance Art (2008), and Collaborative Governance: Private Roles for Public Goals (2011). Apart from academics, Zeckhauser is a Senior Principal at Equity Resource Investments, a real estate private equity firm. He has won multiple national championships in contract bridge. result.main_content_en: The article profiles Richard Zeckhauser, the Frank P. Ramsey Professor of Political Economy at Harvard University's Kennedy School. It details his academic credentials, including his summa cum laude graduation and Ph.D. from Harvard, and his election as a fellow to several prestigious societies. His significant contributions to decision theory and behavioral economics are highlighted, including the development of concepts like quality-adjusted life years (QALYs), status quo bias, and betrayal aversion. His policy work focuses on improving human health, enhancing market efficiency, and fostering better decision-making by individuals and governments. Beyond academia, Zeckhauser is noted for his role as a Senior Principal at a real estate private equity firm and his achievements as a national champion in contract bridge.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了哈佛大学肯尼迪学院弗兰克·P·拉姆齐政治经济学教授理查德·泽克豪泽。文章详述了他的学术背景，包括以最优等成绩从哈佛学院毕业并获得哈佛大学博士学位，以及他当选为计量经济学会、美国国家医学院和美国艺术与科学院院士等荣誉。文章重点强调了他对决策理论和行为经济学的重大贡献，包括提出了质量调整生命年、现状偏见、背叛厌恶等关键概念。他的政策研究致力于促进人类健康、提高市场效率以及推动个人和政府机构做出更明智的决策。除了学术成就，文章还提及了他在一家房地产私募股权公司担任高级负责人的职业角色，以及他作为全国桥牌冠军的成就。
keywords_en: American Academy of Arts and Sciences
